PLTFS RESPONSE TO DEFTS DISCOVERY MOTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF LOPEZ, JOSE

DISCOVERY 302, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CCSF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND INSPECTION DEMANDS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S FAXED DISCOVERY RESPONSES WERE NOT EFFECTIVE SERVICE OF THE SAME ABSENT A WRITTEN AGREEMENT FOR SERVICE OF PAPERS BY FACSIMILE TRANSMISSION. (CODE CIV. PROC., 1013, SUBD. (E).) THERE WAS NO SUCH WRITTEN AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO SERVE THE ORIGINAL RESPONSES BY MAIL OR OTHER MEANS IS THUS TREATED AS A FAILURE TO RESPOND AT ALL TO DEFENDANTS' INTERROGATORIES AND INSPECTION DEMANDS. THEREFORE, WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, TO EACH AND ALL OF DEFENDANTS' FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, AND INSPECTION DEMANDS. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $500.00, ASSESSED AGAINST AND PAYABLE BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. CCSF IS TO PREPARE THE ORDER. JIM WEIXEL, JUDGE PRO-TEM, JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, CLERK, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Ntc Of Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Inspection Demands And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CCSF'S Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Inspection Demands And Request For Monetary Sanctions is to be heard at 9:00 am on the Discovery calendar. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

Defendant Ccsf'S Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Inspection Demands And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Leila K. Mongan, City and County of San Francisco, 415.554.3915, appearing for the Defendant City and County of San Francisco;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant CCSF'S Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Inspection Demands And Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff's faxed discovery responses were not effective service of the same absent a written agreement for service of papers by facsimile transmission. (Code Civ. Proc., 1013, subd. (e).) There was no such written agreement. Plaintiff's failure to serve the original responses by mail or other means is thus treated as a failure to respond at all to Defendants' interrogatories and inspection demands. Therefore, within 20 days of this order, Plaintiff shall serve full and complete responses, without objections, to each and all of Defendants' form interrogatories, special interrogatories, and inspection demands. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $500.00, assessed against and payable by Plaintiff's counsel to Defendant's counsel within 20 days of the date of this order. CCSF is to prepare the order. Jim Weixel, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT
